rationale: X surfaces a genuine, actionable defect (masked/varlen path calling flash_attn_varlen_func when only flash_attn_func may be present, causing a TypeError) that Y actually reviewed and incorrectly cleared as 'correct routing' in its validation sweep — a real miss given GT reviewers (RuixiangMa) flagged this exact class of None-callable crash risk. X's headline finding (#1, version=1/2 'silently swallowed') is contradicted by the PR author's GT reply explaining these are valid branch identifiers, which hurts its precision since it's presented as the top correctness issue. Y is more disciplined and free of fabrication, with solid, well-evidenced findings (test-path coverage gaps, FA3 untested on L4, duplication), but its explicit dismissal of the except-Exception and masked-path patterns as safe misses concerns GT reviewers explicitly raised, so recall and net usefulness land slightly behind X. Both are comparably actionable with concrete file/line references and clear fixes; neither includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
